title: 1.1: Identifies folder-based consumption directory as a document entry point (e.g., mentions document_consumer management command or folder watcher)
1.2: Identifies REST API upload endpoint as a document entry point (e.g., views.py POST handler enqueueing consume_file task)
1.3: Identifies email attachment processing as a document entry point (e.g., paperless_mail.mail module fetching IMAP attachments)
1.5: Identifies signal handlers that run after document storage for enrichment (e.g., mentions add_inbox_tags, set_correspondent, set_document_type, set_tags, add_to_index)
1.6: Identifies Django-Q as the background task execution system (e.g., mentions django-q, Q_CLUSTER, or async_task function)

1.7: Lists document metadata fields that are stored in the database (e.g., mentions id, checksum, mime_type, content, title, correspondent, document_type, tags, created, added, storage_type, filename)
1.8: Identifies required metadata fields with null=False, blank=False constraint (e.g., checksum, mime_type, created, storage_type, added)
1.9: Identifies optional metadata fields with null=True or blank=True constraint (e.g., correspondent, document_type, title, content, archive_checksum)
1.10: Identifies runtime-derived properties that are not stored in the database (e.g., source_path, archive_path, thumbnail_path as @property methods)
1.11: Shows a runtime example with document field values (e.g., displays ID, checksum, MIME type, correspondent, tags, and created timestamp from a document query)
1.12: States the database relationship types for organizational elements (e.g., 'Correspondent uses ForeignKey, Document Type uses ForeignKey, and Tags use ManyToManyField')
